---
title: "04.15_assignment1"
author: "Ashley Marietta"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## IMPORT DATA
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(readxl)
metrics <- read_excel("04.15_metrics.xlsx")
summary(metrics)
```
## MELT DATA TO LONG FORM
```{r}
# convert to data.table for melting
dt <- as.data.table(metrics)

# scale 1-5 variables to 0-1 scale of binary variables
dt[,`Wake Up (Scaled)` := (`Wake Up` - 1) / 4]
dt[,`Productivity (Scaled)` := (Productivity - 1) / 4]

# melt data to long form, excluding Sleep Hours (different scale)
plot_dat <- melt(dt,
                 id.vars = c("Date", "Day"),
                 measure.vars = c("Wake Up (Scaled)", "Productivity (Scaled)", "Caffeine Intake", "Eat Late Night Before"),
                 variable.name = "Metric",
                 value.name = "value")
```

```{r}
# create plot
g_1 <- ggplot(plot_dat,
              aes(x = Date, y = value, color = Metric)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2, height = 0.1, aes(fill = Metric)) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Scaled Metrics - 2026",
       color = "Metric") +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal()

g_1
```

## BUILD VISUALIZATIONS
# CAFFEINE INTAKE VS. PRODUCTIVITY
```{r}
# really not descriptive
g_2 <- ggplot(metrics, aes(x = factor(`Caffeine Intake`), y = Productivity)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Caffeine Intake (0 = No, 1 = Yes)",
       y = "Productivity",
       title = "Productivity vs. Caffeine Intake") +
  theme_minimal()

g_2
```

# SLEEP HOURS VS. PRODUCTIVITY
```{r}
# create scaled versions
dt[, `Sleep Hours (Scaled)` := (`Sleep Hours` - min(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                    (max(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE) - min(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE))]

dt[, `Productivity (Scaled)` := (Productivity - min(Productivity, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                            (max(Productivity, na.rm = TRUE) - min(Productivity, na.rm = TRUE))]

# reshape
sleepvprod <- melt(
  dt,
  id.vars = "Date",
  measure.vars = c("Sleep Hours (Scaled)", "Productivity (Scaled)"),
  variable.name = "Metric",
  value.name = "value"
)

# plot
g_3 <- ggplot(sleepvprod,
              aes(x = Date, y = value, color = Metric)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Sleep Hours vs. Productivity (Scaled)",
       y = "Value",
       color = "Metric") +
  theme_minimal()

g_3
```

# SLEEP HOURS VS. ENERGY
```{r}
# create scaled versions
dt[, `Sleep Hours (Scaled)` := (`Sleep Hours` - min(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                    (max(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE) - min(`Sleep Hours`, na.rm = TRUE))]

dt[, `Wake Up (Scaled)` := (`Wake Up` - min(`Wake Up`, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                            (max(`Wake Up`, na.rm = TRUE) - min(`Wake Up`, na.rm = TRUE))]

# reshape
sleepvwup <- melt(
  dt,
  id.vars = "Date",
  measure.vars = c("Sleep Hours (Scaled)", "Wake Up (Scaled)"),
  variable.name = "Metric",
  value.name = "value"
)

# plot
g_4 <- ggplot(sleepvwup,
              aes(x = Date, y = value, color = Metric)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Sleep Hours vs. Wake Up Energy (Scaled)",
       y = "Value",
       color = "Metric") +
  theme_minimal()

g_4
```
